mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest or the signal loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in housing starts indicates a consumer spending shift away from durable goods, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive returns in >60% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during tightening cycles or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.5 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in >50% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power when inflation is below 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative returns in >55% of observations or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic strength that translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals impending recessionary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces positive returns in >50% of late-cycle contraction periods or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 5-year sample.
